port_banners.80:
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request Date: Sat, 16 May 2026 16:57:25 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.7 (Ubuntu) Content-Length: 427 Connection: close Content-Type: text/html; charset=iso-8859-1 400 Bad Request

Bad Request

Your browser sent a request that this server could not understand.

Additionally, a 400 Bad Request error was encountered while trying to use an ErrorDocument to handle the request.

Apache/2.4.7 (Ubuntu) Server at scanme.nmap.org Port 80

configuration_findings.title: Multiple security headers missing (5)
configuration_findings.recommended_action: Harden HTTP response headers per organizational baseline

configuration_findings.title: Site served over cleartext HTTP without HTTPS in scope
configuration_findings.recommended_action: Enable HTTPS and redirect HTTP to HTTPS

configuration_findings.title: OpenSSH 6.6.1p1 is end-of-life / unsupported
configuration_findings.recommended_action: Upgrade OpenSSH to a vendor-supported release

vulnerabilities.summary: The kbdint_next_device function in auth2-chall.c in sshd in OpenSSH through 6.9 does not properly restrict the processing of keyboard-interactive devices within a single connection, which makes it easier for remote attackers to conduct brute-force attacks or cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via a long and duplicative list in the ssh -oKbdInteractiveDevices option, as demonstrated by a m
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (6.9 and earlier)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (6.9 and earlier)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.99; CVSS base 8.5; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (6.9 and earlier))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: Certain Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) 4 and 5 packages for OpenSSH, as signed in August 2008 using a legitimate Red Hat GPG key, contain an externally introduced modification (Trojan Horse) that allows the package authors to have an unknown impact.  NOTE: since the malicious packages were not distributed from any official Red Hat sources, the scope of this issue is restricted to users who may ha
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.86; CVSS base 9.3; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product)
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: Untrusted search path vulnerability in ssh-agent.c in ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 7.4 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary local PKCS#11 modules by leveraging control over a forwarded agent-socket.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.79; CVSS base 7.3; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

vulnerabilities.summary: Use-after-free vulnerability in the mm_answer_pam_free_ctx function in monitor.c in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.0 on non-OpenBSD platforms might allow local users to gain privileges by leveraging control of the sshd uid to send an unexpectedly early MONITOR_REQ_PAM_FREE_CTX request.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.0)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.0)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.84; CVSS base 6.9; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.0))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

vulnerabilities.summary: The x11_open_helper function in channels.c in ssh in OpenSSH before 6.9, when ForwardX11Trusted mode is not used, lacks a check of the refusal deadline for X connections, which makes it easier for remote attackers to bypass intended access restrictions via a connection outside of the permitted time window.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 6.9)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 6.9)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.90; CVSS base 4.3; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 6.9))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: The shared memory manager (associated with pre-authentication compression) in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.4 does not ensure that a bounds check is enforced by all compilers, which might allows local users to gain privileges by leveraging access to a sandboxed privilege-separation process, related to the m_zback and m_zlib data structures.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.06; CVSS base 7.8; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

vulnerabilities.summary: sshd in OpenSSH before 7.4, when privilege separation is not used, creates forwarded Unix-domain sockets as root, which might allow local users to gain privileges via unspecified vectors, related to serverloop.c.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.25; CVSS base 7.0; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

vulnerabilities.summary: authfile.c in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.4 does not properly consider the effects of realloc on buffer contents, which might allow local users to obtain sensitive private-key information by leveraging access to a privilege-separated child process.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.04; CVSS base 5.5; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

vulnerabilities.summary: OpenSSH, when using OPIE (One-Time Passwords in Everything) for PAM, allows remote attackers to determine the existence of certain user accounts, which displays a different response if the user account exists and is configured to use one-time passwords (OTP), a similar issue to CVE-2007-2243.
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.40; CVSS base 4.3; match confidence: corroborated; relevance: high (banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product)
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

vulnerabilities.summary: The monitor component in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.0 on non-OpenBSD platforms accepts extraneous username data in MONITOR_REQ_PAM_INIT_CTX requests, which allows local users to conduct impersonation attacks by leveraging any SSH login access in conjunction with control of the sshd uid to send a crafted MONITOR_REQ_PWNAM request, related to monitor.c and monitor_wrap.c.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.0)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.0)

action_queue.summary: Use-after-free vulnerability in the mm_answer_pam_free_ctx function in monitor.c in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.0 on non-OpenBSD platforms might allow local users to gain privileges by leveraging control of the sshd uid to send an unexpectedly early MONITOR_REQ_PAM_FREE_CTX request.

action_queue.summary: The x11_open_helper function in channels.c in ssh in OpenSSH before 6.9, when ForwardX11Trusted mode is not used, lacks a check of the refusal deadline for X connections, which makes it easier for remote attackers to bypass intended access restrictions via a connection outside of the permitted time window.

action_queue.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

action_queue.summary: The shared memory manager (associated with pre-authentication compression) in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.4 does not ensure that a bounds check is enforced by all compilers, which might allows local users to gain privileges by leveraging access to a sandboxed privilege-separation process, related to the m_zback and m_zlib data structures.

data.summary: OpenSSH 6.6p1 through 7.5 suffers from a user enumeration vulnerability due to timing discrepancies when checking valid users.
OpenSSH 6.6p1 through 6.9p1 contains a flaw in the kex_input_kexinit function that could allow a man-in-the-middle attacker to bypass the MaxAuthTries limit.
OpenSSH 6.6p1 and earlier allows remote authenticated users to cause a denial of service via crafted network traffic.
OpenSSH 6.6p1 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via a flood of TCP connection requests.
OpenSSH 6.6p1 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption or crash) via a crafted packet.
OpenSSH 6.6p1 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU and memory consumption) via a flood of connection attempts.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 and earlier may allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted HTTP request.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted HTTP request.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted HTTP request.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.7 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted HTTP request.
